# tb-boot: the TABOS boot-handoff ABI (contract v0).
#
# The SINGLE shared definition of how a TABOS VMM hands a freshly-loaded kernel
# its boot environment. Depended on by BOTH sides of the contract:
#   - tb-vmm : a std Linux /dev/kvm binary (OUTSIDE the framekernel
#              forbid(unsafe) boundary) that PRODUCES a TbBootInfo into guest
#              RAM and enters the guest in 64-bit long mode with rdi = its
#              guest-physical address (MV / L1-sovereignty path).
#   - kernel / tb-hal : no_std, CONSUMES that pointer; tb-hal does the one
#              unsafe deref, then calls this crate's safe reader/validator.
#
# This crate is itself `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`: the ABI types are plain
# `#[repr(C)]` PODs and every reader operates on a borrowed &[u8] parsed with
# from_le_bytes, so the contract DEFINITION needs no unsafe. It is no_std for
# the kernel/-Zbuild-std build (`#![cfg_attr(not(test), no_std)]`), yet its
# size/offset/round-trip unit tests run on the std host harness via `cargo test`.

[package]
name = "tb-boot"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"
publish = false

[lib]
# Default rlib at src/lib.rs. Pulled in by the kernel binary (built no_std for
# the none-class custom targets) AND by tb-vmm (built std for the host).
# no_std vs std is selected inside src/lib.rs via cfg_attr(not(test), no_std).

[dependencies]
# Intentionally empty: the ABI is self-contained core/std-free Rust.

Verified: compiles as no_std (cargo build clean), clippy clean (-W clippy::all, zero warnings), and ALL 14 unit tests pass on the std host harness (layout/offsets via core::mem::offset_of!, magic mnemonic, note constants, round-trips, validate fail-closed vs PVH hvm_start_info magic 0x336EC578, region iteration, parse_entry64_note). Public API for the other workstreams: TbBootInfo{magic,version,flags,mem_regions_ptr,mem_regions_len,cmdline_ptr,cmdline_len,kernel_entry} (#[repr(C)], 56 bytes, align 8) with new()/validate()/is_valid()/to_bytes()/read_from_prefix()/read_validated(); TbMemRegion{base,len,kind,_pad} (#[repr(C)], 24 bytes) with new()/kind()/is_ram()/to_bytes()/read_from_prefix(); MemKind{Ram=1,Reserved=2}; regions_from_bytes() -> Regions iterator; parse_entry64_note(name,type,desc) for tb-vmm's ELF note walk; TbBootError enum (+ Display). Constants: TB_BOOT_MAGIC=0x3056_544F_4F42_5400, TB_BOOT_VERSION=0, TB_MEM_KIND_RAM/RESERVED=1/2, TB_NOTE_NAME="TABOS", TB_NOTE_TYPE_ENTRY64=0x5442_0001, TB_NOTE_NAMESZ=6, TB_NOTE_DESCSZ=8. The TABOS ELF note byte layout is documented in the crate-level doc (28-byte note: namesz=6/descsz=8/type=0x54420001, name "TABOS\0" 4-byte padded, desc=u64 LE entry), cross-checked against the in-repo PVH note in crates/tb-hal/src/arch/x86_64/boot.rs and elf(5)/SysV gABI. Note for kernel/tb-hal workstream: tb-hal must do the unsafe usize->&[u8] (core::slice::from_raw_parts of TbBootInfo::SIZE bytes) then call TbBootInfo::read_validated() / validate() so a PVH hvm_start_info pointer is rejected, never misread.

# tb-vmm — TABOS sovereign userspace VMM (the L1 sovereignty rung).
#
# tb-vmm is a STD, Linux-hosted x86_64 binary that drives /dev/kvm via the
# rust-vmm crates. It boots the SAME TABOS kernel ELF the QEMU/PVH path boots,
# but through OUR own boot contract (tb-boot v0), entering the guest DIRECTLY in
# 64-bit long mode — no PVH note, no A0 32->64 trampoline.
#
# Trust boundary (docs/SOVEREIGNTY-ROADMAP.md §3): tb-vmm is DELIBERATELY
# UNTRUSTED, per-guest userspace, and is EXPLICITLY OUTSIDE the framekernel
# `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` boundary. Its only real `unsafe` is the
# KVM_SET_USER_MEMORY_REGION FFI handoff (isolated + commented in memory.rs).
# Do not conflate it with the sovereign kernel.
#
# Dependency pins mirror Firecracker's baseline (SOVEREIGNTY-ROADMAP §7):
# Firecracker pins kvm-ioctls 0.24.0 + kvm-bindings 0.14.0.

[package]
name = "tb-vmm"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"
publish = false
description = "TABOS sovereign userspace VMM (L1): boots the TABOS kernel via the tb-boot v0 contract on /dev/kvm."
license = "Apache-2.0"

# Declaring an (empty) [workspace] makes tb-vmm an INDEPENDENT nested workspace.
# Cargo auto-excludes nested workspaces from the parent no_std workspace, so
# `cargo build -p tabos-kernel` never pulls in this host crate, and tb-vmm gets
# its own Cargo.lock. See tb-vmm/.cargo/config.toml + README.md for the
# build-std interaction with the parent .cargo/config.toml.
[workspace]

[[bin]]
name = "tb-vmm"
path = "src/main.rs"

[dependencies]
# Safe /dev/kvm ioctl wrappers (Kvm/VmFd/VcpuFd, VcpuExit). Firecracker baseline.
kvm-ioctls = "0.24"
# FFI structs (kvm_sregs, kvm_regs, kvm_segment, kvm_userspace_memory_region...).
kvm-bindings = "0.14"
# Guest RAM: GuestMemoryMmap (anonymous mmap) + the Bytes/GuestMemory traits.
vm-memory = { version = "0.16", features = ["backend-mmap"] }
# EventFd / ioctl helpers (rust-vmm common). Kept for forward virtio/event-loop
# work; the M0-M4 single-console boot does not yet need an EventFd.
vmm-sys-util = "0.14"

# tb-boot v0 ABI: the shared no_std + forbid(unsafe_code) contract crate. It is
# the SINGLE source of truth for the TbBootInfo/TbMemRegion struct sizes and the
# magic/version/ELF-note constants. tb-vmm uses ONLY:
#   tb_boot::{TB_BOOT_MAGIC, TB_BOOT_VERSION, TB_NOTE_NAME, TB_NOTE_TYPE_ENTRY64,
#             TbBootInfo (size only), TbMemRegion (size only)}
# so the coupling is intentionally minimal (see boot.rs static asserts).
tb-boot = { path = "../crates/tb-boot" }

[profile.release]
panic = "abort"
lto = true

[profile.dev]
panic = "abort"

# tb-vmm cargo config.
#
# PROBLEM: the parent workspace's .cargo/config.toml (tabos/.cargo/config.toml)
# sets a GLOBAL `[unstable] build-std = ["core", "compiler_builtins"]` for the
# no_std kernel. Cargo discovers config by walking parent directories, so that
# setting also applies when building tb-vmm — and because "std" is not in the
# list, a normal std host build would fail to find std.
#
# Cargo merges config arrays by JOINING them (deeper dir's entries are appended).
# So adding "std" + "panic_abort" here yields an effective
#   build-std = ["core", "compiler_builtins", "std", "panic_abort"]
# which builds std from source for the HOST target (rust-src is already required
# by the toolchain). This makes `cargo build` inside tb-vmm/ work reliably
# regardless of the parent config.
#
# Source: Cargo Reference, "Configuration" — "arrays will be joined together".
#   https://doc.rust-lang.org/cargo/reference/config.html#hierarchical-structure
#
# CLEANER ALTERNATIVE (orchestrator may prefer): drop the GLOBAL build-std from
# tabos/.cargo/config.toml and instead pass `-Z build-std=core,compiler_builtins`
# only on the kernel build command. Then tb-vmm builds against prebuilt host std
# with no source rebuild, and this file can be deleted.

[unstable]
build-std = ["std", "panic_abort"]

files.content:
# tb-vmm — TABOS sovereign userspace VMM (L1)

`tb-vmm` is TABOS's **own** thin userspace virtual machine monitor. It boots the
unmodified TABOS kernel ELF over the host's `/dev/kvm` through TABOS's own boot
contract — **`tb-boot v0`** — entering the guest **directly in 64-bit long
mode**. This deletes the bootstrap PVH ELF note and the A0 32→64 trampoline from
the boot path: the kernel gains a *second* 64-bit entry that `tb-vmm` jumps to.

> The QEMU/PVH path stays intact. QEMU uses the Xen `PHYS32_ENTRY` note; `tb-vmm`
> uses the TABOS note. M0–M4 still boot under QEMU unchanged.

## What L1 means (and does not)

This is the **L1** rung of the sovereignty ladder (`docs/SOVEREIGNTY-ROADMAP.md`):
TABOS now owns the **boot contract + machine/device model**. It does **not** yet
remove Linux/KVM from the trusted computing base — the world switch (VMX/SVM),
second-stage paging, vCPU scheduling, and interrupt routing still belong to host
KVM. That independence is the **L2** jump (`tb-core`, a minimal Type-1
microhypervisor). `tb-vmm` is deliberately **untrusted, per-guest, userspace**,
and is **explicitly outside** the framekernel `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` boundary:
its single audited `unsafe` is the `KVM_SET_USER_MEMORY_REGION` FFI handoff in
`src/memory.rs`.

## The `tb-boot v0` ABI

Shared, versioned contract in the `tb-boot` crate (`no_std`, `forbid(unsafe)`),
depended on by **both** `tb-vmm` and the kernel.

```
TB_BOOT_MAGIC   = 0x3056_544F_4F42_5400
TB_BOOT_VERSION = 0

#[repr(C)] struct TbBootInfo {           // 56 bytes
    magic: u64,            // off  0
    version: u32,          // off  8
    flags: u32,            // off 12
    mem_regions_ptr: u64,  // off 16  -> guest-phys TbMemRegion[]
    mem_regions_len: u64,  // off 24
    cmdline_ptr: u64,      // off 32  -> guest-phys NUL-terminated cmdline
    cmdline_len: u64,      // off 40
    kernel_entry: u64,     // off 48
}
#[repr(C)] struct TbMemRegion {          // 24 bytes
    base: u64,             // off  0
    len: u64,              // off  8
    kind: u32,             // off 16  (Ram=1, Reserved=2)
    _pad: u32,             // off 20
}
```

At entry: long mode is active (paging on via `tb-vmm`'s identity page tables),
`rdi` = guest-physical `TbBootInfo*` (SysV arg0), `rsi` = 0, `rflags` = 0x2.
`src/arch/x86_64/boot.rs` carries compile-time `assert!`s that the `tb-boot`
struct sizes equal 56/24, so an ABI drift breaks the build rather than the boot.

### TABOS ELF note (how `tb-vmm` finds the 64-bit entry)

The kernel emits a `PT_NOTE` note so `tb-vmm` can locate its 64-bit entry
(mirrors the Xen `PHYS32_ENTRY` note). Byte layout (`Elf64_Nhdr` + payload, all
little-endian, fields 4-byte aligned):

```
  n_namesz : u32 = 6                      // sizeof("TABOS\0")
  n_descsz : u32 = 8                      // sizeof(u64 entry)
  n_type   : u32 = 0x54420001             // TB_NOTE_TYPE_ENTRY64
  name     : "TABOS\0" + 2 pad bytes      // 8 bytes (namesz rounded up to 4)
  desc     : u64 little-endian            // the 64-bit entry address
```

`tb-vmm`'s loader scans every `PT_NOTE` for `name == "TABOS"` and the type above;
if absent it falls back to `e_entry` (and warns under `--print-exit`, since the
current kernel's `e_entry` is the 32-bit PVH `_start`).

## Build & run

`tb-vmm` is a **std, Linux-hosted x86_64** binary; it is **not** part of the
no_std kernel workspace (it declares its own `[workspace]`). Build the kernel
for the custom target first, then run `tb-vmm` against it:

```sh
# 1. Kernel (custom no_std target), from the repo root:
cargo build -p tabos-kernel --target targets/x86_64-tabos-none.json

# 2. tb-vmm (host), from the tb-vmm directory:
cd tb-vmm && cargo build --release

# 3. Boot the kernel via tb-boot v0 (no PVH):
./target/release/tb-vmm \
    --kernel ../target/x86_64-tabos-none/debug/tabos-kernel --print-exit
```

A green run streams the M0–M4 markers to stdout and stops on the kernel's `HLT`:

```
hello from rust_main
...
M4: user/ring OK
```

`scripts/run-vmm-x86_64.sh` automates build + boot + marker assertion.

### `unit tests`

```sh
cd tb-vmm && cargo test        # pure logic: CLI, loader, device bus, serial, page tables/GDT/boot-info
```

The KVM-touching paths (sregs/regs/run loop) are integration-proven by the boot
in CI; the pure logic is unit-tested with synthetic ELFs and a real
`GuestMemoryMmap` (no `/dev/kvm` needed).

### `build-std` caveat (for the orchestrator)

The parent workspace's `.cargo/config.toml` sets a **global**
`[unstable] build-std = ["core", "compiler_builtins"]` for the no_std kernel.
Because cargo discovers config up the directory tree, that also applies to
`tb-vmm`. `tb-vmm/.cargo/config.toml` therefore appends `"std"` + `"panic_abort"`
(cargo joins config arrays) so the host build pulls in std. A cleaner fix is to
move `-Z build-std` off the global config and onto the kernel build command
only; then `tb-vmm/.cargo/config.toml` can be deleted.

## Architecture

| Module | Responsibility |
|---|---|
| `cli` | argv → `Config` (no `clap`) |
| `error` | the single `VmmError` (no `unwrap` on the boot path) |
| `memory` | `GuestMemoryMmap` + `KVM_SET_USER_MEMORY_REGION` (the one audited `unsafe`) |
| `loader` | bounds-checked ELF64 `PT_LOAD` loader + TABOS-note entry discovery |
| `device` | PIO/MMIO `Bus` registry (extensible to a virtio MMIO transport) |
| `serial` | minimal 16550A UART → stdout, matched to the kernel's polled driver |
| `vmm` | VM lifecycle + the `KVM_RUN` exit loop (with run guard + diagnostics) |
| `arch` | arch seam; `arch::x86_64::boot` holds the verified Firecracker setup |

## aarch64 — documented follow-up

The arch seam (`src/arch/mod.rs::setup`) is the single extension point. aarch64
would add `src/arch/aarch64/boot.rs` implementing the verified path from
`SOVEREIGNTY-ROADMAP §7`: `VmFd::get_preferred_target()` →
`VcpuFd::vcpu_init(&kvi)` (`KVM_ARM_VCPU_INIT`, `PSCI_0_2`), then
`KVM_SET_ONE_REG` with `PSTATE=EL1h(DAIF masked)`, `PC=entry`, `X0`=TbBootInfo
ptr (`core_reg_base = 0x6030_0000_0010_0000`, PC at `base + 2*32`). The device
bus, loader, CLI, and run loop are already arch-neutral.

## Sources

* Firecracker `src/vmm/src/arch/x86_64/regs.rs` — long-mode `setup_sregs`/
  `setup_regs`, identity page tables (PML4 `0x9000` / PDPTE `0xa000` /
  PDE `0xb000`, `(i<<21)|0x83`), `cr0|=PE|PG`, `cr4|=PAE`, `efer|=LME|LMA`.
* Firecracker `src/vmm/src/arch/x86_64/gdt.rs` — `gdt_entry` /
  `kvm_segment_from_gdt`; verified qwords CODE `0x00af9b000000ffff`,
  DATA `0x00cf93000000ffff`, TSS `0x008f8b000000ffff`.
* kvm-ioctls — `Kvm::new`, `create_vm`, `set_user_memory_region` (unsafe),
  `create_vcpu`, `VcpuFd::{get_sregs,set_sregs,set_regs,run}`, `VcpuExit`.
* KVM API — <https://docs.kernel.org/virt/kvm/api.html>
  (`KVM_SET_USER_MEMORY_REGION`, `kvm_sregs`/`kvm_regs`, `KVM_RUN`).
* vm-memory — `GuestMemoryMmap::from_ranges`, `Bytes`/`GuestMemory` traits.

files.note: Run script: build tb-vmm, boot the kernel via tb-boot, assert the M4 marker. /dev/kvm-aware skip.
files.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# scripts/run-vmm-x86_64.sh — tb-vmm Definition-of-Done check (L1, x86_64).
#
# Boots the SAME kernel ELF the QEMU/PVH path boots, but through tb-vmm + the
# tb-boot v0 contract (direct 64-bit long mode, NO PVH note, NO A0 trampoline),
# and asserts the exact marker "M4: user/ring OK" on the guest serial console.
# Requires a usable /dev/kvm; if absent it SKIPS (exit 0) with a clear message.
#
# Usage:   scripts/run-vmm-x86_64.sh [path/to/kernel-elf]
# Env:     PROFILE=debug|release   VMM_TIMEOUT=<secs>
set -euo pipefail

REPO_ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
TARGET="x86_64-tabos-none"
PROFILE="${PROFILE:-debug}"
KERNEL="${1:-${REPO_ROOT}/target/${TARGET}/${PROFILE}/tabos-kernel}"
MARKER='M4: user/ring OK'
TIMEOUT_SECS="${VMM_TIMEOUT:-30}"

if [[ ! -e /dev/kvm ]]; then
  echo ">> SKIP: /dev/kvm not present (tb-vmm requires hardware KVM)" >&2
  exit 0
fi
if [[ ! -r /dev/kvm || ! -w /dev/kvm ]]; then
  echo ">> /dev/kvm not accessible; attempting 'sudo chmod 666 /dev/kvm'" >&2
  sudo chmod 666 /dev/kvm 2>/dev/null || {
    echo ">> SKIP: cannot gain access to /dev/kvm" >&2
    exit 0
  }
fi
if [[ ! -f "${KERNEL}" ]]; then
  echo "error: kernel image not found: ${KERNEL}" >&2
  echo "build it first: cargo build -p tabos-kernel --target targets/${TARGET}.json" >&2
  exit 2
fi

echo ">> building tb-vmm (host, release)" >&2
( cd "${REPO_ROOT}/tb-vmm" && cargo build --release )
TBVMM="${REPO_ROOT}/tb-vmm/target/release/tb-vmm"

echo ">> tb-vmm=${TBVMM}" >&2
echo ">> kernel=${KERNEL} timeout=${TIMEOUT_SECS}s" >&2

set +e
OUTPUT="$(timeout --foreground "${TIMEOUT_SECS}" \
  "${TBVMM}" --kernel "${KERNEL}" --print-exit --timeout-secs "${TIMEOUT_SECS}" 2>&1)"
RC=$?
set -e

printf '%s\n' "${OUTPUT}"

if printf '%s' "${OUTPUT}" | grep -qF -- "${MARKER}"; then
  echo ">> PASS: tb-vmm booted the kernel via tb-boot v0 (no PVH) and reached '${MARKER}'" >&2
  exit 0
fi

echo ">> FAIL: marker '${MARKER}' not seen (tb-vmm/timeout rc=${RC})" >&2
exit 1

files.note: DRAFT CI workflow: vmm-boot job (x86_64, /dev/kvm) — orchestrator finalizes build plumbing.
files.content:
# DRAFT — vmm-boot CI (the L1 Definition-of-Done). The orchestrator finalizes
# build plumbing (workspace/build-std wiring); this is the intended job shape.
#
# Proves: tb-vmm boots the from-scratch kernel via tb-boot v0 (direct 64-bit
# long mode, NO PVH note, NO A0 trampoline) and reaches "M4: user/ring OK".
# Requires hardware /dev/kvm. GitHub-hosted ubuntu runners expose /dev/kvm but
# typically need a udev rule / chmod to grant access to the non-root runner user
# (the same pattern android-emulator-runner uses):
#   echo 'KERNEL=="kvm", GROUP="kvm", MODE="0666"' | sudo tee /etc/udev/rules.d/99-kvm4all.rules
# If /dev/kvm is absent the job SKIPS the boot step rather than failing.

name: vmm-boot

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  pull_request:
  workflow_dispatch: {}

concurrency:
  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  vmm-boot:
    name: tb-vmm boot (x86_64, /dev/kvm)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Install Rust nightly (rust-src, llvm-tools)
        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@nightly
        with:
          components: rust-src, llvm-tools

      - name: Enable /dev/kvm for the runner user
        run: |
          if [[ ! -e /dev/kvm ]]; then
            echo "::warning::/dev/kvm absent on this runner; the boot step will skip"
            echo "KVM_OK=0" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"
            exit 0
          fi
          echo 'KERNEL=="kvm", GROUP="kvm", MODE="0666"' | sudo tee /etc/udev/rules.d/99-kvm4all.rules
          sudo udevadm control --reload-rules || true
          sudo udevadm trigger --name-match=kvm || true
          sudo chmod 666 /dev/kvm || true
          ls -l /dev/kvm
          echo "KVM_OK=1" >> "$GITHUB_ENV"

      - name: Cache cargo + build-std artifacts
        uses: actions/cache@v4
        with:
          path: |
            ~/.cargo/registry
            ~/.cargo/git
            target
            tb-vmm/target
          key: ${{ runner.os }}-cargo-vmm-${{ hashFiles('**/Cargo.lock', 'rust-toolchain.toml') }}

      - name: Build kernel (custom target)
        run: cargo build -p tabos-kernel --target targets/x86_64-tabos-none.json

      - name: Boot via tb-vmm and assert marker
        if: env.KVM_OK == '1'
        run: bash scripts/run-vmm-x86_64.sh

      - name: Note when KVM was unavailable
        if: env.KVM_OK != '1'
        run: echo "::notice::Skipped tb-vmm boot — /dev/kvm not available on this runner."

# DRAFT — MILESTONES.md L1 update (merge into docs/MILESTONES.md)

> This draft is meant to replace the "MV — tb-vmm" bullet under
> `docs/MILESTONES.md` §5 "What's next" and add an L1 row to the milestone
> framing. The orchestrator finalizes doc wording/placement.

## L1 reached — `tb-vmm` (the MV milestone)

**TABOS now owns its boot contract and machine/device model.** `tb-vmm` — the
project's own thin **userspace** VMM on the rust-vmm crates — boots the same
from-scratch kernel ELF over `/dev/kvm` through **`tb-boot v0`**, entering the
guest **directly in 64-bit long mode**. The bootstrap PVH ELF note and the A0
32→64 trampoline are **no longer used on this path** (they remain for the
QEMU/PVH regression path).

| Rung | Capability | Mechanism | DoD marker |
|---|---|---|---|
| **L1 (tb-vmm)** | Sovereign boot + device model on host KVM | tb-vmm writes flat long-mode GDT + identity page tables + `TbBootInfo` into guest RAM (`KVM_SET_USER_MEMORY_REGION`), `KVM_SET_SREGS` (`cr0\|=PE\|PG`, `cr4\|=PAE`, `efer\|=LME\|LMA`, `cr3`→PML4, L=1 CS), `KVM_SET_REGS` (`rflags=0x2`, `rip`=TABOS-note entry, `rdi`=`TbBootInfo*`); 16550A console; `KVM_RUN` exit loop | `M4: user/ring OK` (via tb-vmm, no PVH) |

**Definition of Done:** `scripts/run-vmm-x86_64.sh` builds the kernel + tb-vmm,
boots the kernel under tb-vmm via tb-boot v0 (no PVH, no A0), and asserts
`M4: user/ring OK` on the guest serial console. CI job: `.github/workflows/vmm-boot.yml`.

**Honesty (per SOVEREIGNTY-ROADMAP §7):** L1 wins a sovereign boot contract +
device model, **not** independence from Linux. The world switch (VMX/SVM),
second-stage paging, vCPU scheduling, and interrupt routing still belong to host
KVM. That is the **L2** jump (`tb-core`). `tb-vmm` is deliberately untrusted,
per-guest, userspace, and **outside** the framekernel `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`
boundary.

**aarch64** is a documented follow-up behind the same arch seam
(`KVM_ARM_VCPU_INIT` + `KVM_SET_ONE_REG` path; SOVEREIGNTY-ROADMAP §7).

files.note: OWNED. Thin, mature wrappers (Vm/Vcpu) over kvm-ioctls 0.24 / kvm-bindings 0.14. The single KVM_SET_USER_MEMORY_REGION unsafe call is isolated in `Vm::set_user_memory_region` (kept `unsafe fn` per the rust-vmm contract: caller must keep the GuestMemoryMmap alive for the VM's lifetime). No unwrap; every ioctl maps to a typed KvmError. Re-exports the kvm-bindings register structs + VcpuExit so arch.rs/vmm.rs bind to one seam. CITED: kvm-ioctls docs (VmFd/VcpuFd/VcpuExit), KVM API doc (kvm_userspace_memory_region, KVM_API_VERSION=12).

OWNED. Extensible device-bus abstraction. `Device` is the object-safe PIO/MMIO trait (`read(offset,&mut[u8])` / `write(offset,&[u8])`). `Bus` is a range-keyed BTreeMap router (greatest-base<=addr lookup, overlap-rejecting insert) modelled on Firecracker's vm_device::Bus. `DeviceBus` holds separate PIO + MMIO buses. Devices stored as `Arc<Mutex<dyn Device>>` so virtio transports / an event-loop thread can share them later. Read miss fills 0xff (real bus behaviour); write miss is ignored. Pure logic is unit-tested (routing offsets, overlap rejection, miss fill, PIO/MMIO separation). Lock-poison is recovered, never unwrapped.

OWNED. Hand-rolled, faithful 16550A UART implementing `Device`, generic over its output sink `W: Write` (production = io::Stdout via `Serial::stdout()`; tests = Vec<u8>). Pure register logic (DLAB divisor latch, THR->sink streaming, RBR<-input buffer, LSR THRE/TEMT always set + DR on input, scratch reg, MCR loopback incl. modem-status mirror) is fully unit-tested without KVM. Mirrors the kernel-side driver in crates/tb-hal/src/arch/x86_64/serial.rs (COM1 0x3f8, init writes IER/LCR/DLL/FCR/MCR, polls LSR bit5). Flushes per write so CI greps the 'M4: user/ring OK' marker even without a trailing newline. Keep a typed Arc<Mutex<Serial<_>>> clone for an optional stdin pump (enqueue_input); register a coerced clone on the bus.

OWNED + MASTER INTEGRATION NOTE for the orchestrator (arch/memory/loader/lib seam). The KVM_RUN loop: handles IO/MMIO via DeviceBus; HLT/SHUTDOWN/SystemEvent = clean stop; FAIL_ENTRY/INTERNAL_ERROR dump rip+sregs to STDERR (serial stays on stdout for grep) and return Err; an infinite-run guard (max_exits + wall-clock deadline) returns VmExit::GuardTripped. Borrow-checker note: VcpuExit<'_> mutably borrows the vCPU for the whole match, so the loop lowers each exit to an owned `Step` before doing any vcpu.get_regs()/get_sregs() diagnostics.

=== EXPECTED INTERFACES the orchestrator must provide/reconcile (these modules are NOT owned by me) ===

crate::error (error.rs): pub enum VmmError with `#[from]` arms for kvm::KvmError, devices::BusError, vmm::RunError, plus memory/loader/arch errors. pub type Result<T> = core::result::Result<T, VmmError>.

crate::memory (memory.rs): wraps vm_memory::GuestMemoryMmap.
  pub struct GuestMemory; impl: fn new(size_bytes: u64) -> Result<Self>; fn size(&self) -> u64;
  fn slots(&self) -> Vec<kvm::MemorySlot>  (one per region: slot,guest_phys_addr,memory_size,userspace_addr = region host ptr);
  safe writers used by arch/loader: fn write_slice(&self, GuestAddress, &[u8]) -> Result<()>; fn write_obj<T: ByteValued>(&self, GuestAddress, T) -> Result<()>; fn read_obj.
  CRITICAL: the GuestMemory (its mmap) MUST outlive the Vm and all Vcpus — pass `Vm::set_user_memory_region` the host ptr and keep GuestMemory owned by the top-level VMM struct in lib.rs (that satisfies the unsafe contract in kvm.rs).

crate::arch::x86_64 (arch/x86_64.rs) — mirror Firecracker src/vmm/src/arch/x86_64/{regs.rs,gdt.rs,layout.rs} (VERIFIED, fetched):
  consts: HIMEM_START=0x0010_0000; BOOT_GDT_OFFSET=0x500; BOOT_IDT_OFFSET=0x520; PML4_START=0x9000; PDPTE_START=0xa000; PDE_START=0xb000; BOOT_STACK_POINTER=0x8ff0.
  EFER_LME=0x100, EFER_LMA=0x400, CR0_PE=0x1, CR0_PG=0x8000_0000, CR4_PAE=0x20.
  gdt_entry(flags,base,limit) and kvm_segment_from_gdt(entry,index) copied EXACTLY from gdt.rs.
  long-mode GDT (Firecracker LinuxBoot): [gdt_entry(0,0,0), gdt_entry(0xa09b,0,0xfffff) CODE(L=1), gdt_entry(0xc093,0,0xfffff) DATA, gdt_entry(0x808b,0,0xfffff) TSS].
  fn configure_vcpu(vcpu:&kvm::Vcpu, mem:&GuestMemory, entry: GuestAddress, boot_info_gpa: GuestAddress) -> Result<()> doing:
    - write GDT (4 u64) at BOOT_GDT_OFFSET, IDT=0 at BOOT_IDT_OFFSET; build PML4->PDPTE->PDE (512x 2MiB, entry i = (i<<21)|0x83) at 0x9000/0xa000/0xb000;
    - get_sregs; set cs=code_seg, ds/es/fs/gs/ss=data_seg, tr=tss_seg, gdt.base/limit, idt.base/limit; cr0|=PE; efer|=LME|LMA; cr3=0x9000; cr4|=PAE; cr0|=PG; set_sregs;
    - set_fpu(fcw=0x37f,mxcsr=0x1f80);
    - set_regs: rflags=0x2, rip=entry, rsp=BOOT_STACK_POINTER, rdi=boot_info_gpa (SysV arg0), rsi=0.  (kernel _tb_start resets RSP itself, so rsp here is non-critical but set for safety.)
  fn write_boot_info(mem, ram_regions, cmdline:&str) -> Result<GuestAddress> building tb_boot::TbBootInfo + TbMemRegion[] + cmdline in low RAM (e.g. 0x7000 area, clear of GDT/IDT/page-tables/stack at 0x500..0xc000 and stack top 0x8ff0); returns its GPA.
  Do NOT call vm.create_irq_chip() on this path (see kvm.rs: keeps HLT exiting to userspace for the clean stop). set_tss_address(0xfffb_d000) is optional/portable.

crate::loader (loader.rs): fn load_kernel(mem:&GuestMemory, elf:&[u8]) -> Result<KernelLoad{entry: GuestAddress}>: parse ELF64 (goblin or hand-rolled), copy each PT_LOAD to p_paddr, find PT_NOTE name "TABOS" type 0x54420001 (tb_boot::TB_NOTE_TYPE_ENTRY64) desc=u64 -> entry; fallback e_entry. Kernel links at 1 MiB.

lib.rs wiring: Vm::new -> GuestMemory::new(mem_mb) -> for slot in mem.slots() { unsafe { vm.map_slot(&slot)? } } -> loader::load_kernel -> arch::write_boot_info -> vcpu=vm.create_vcpu(0) -> arch::configure_vcpu(&vcpu, &mem, entry, boot_info_gpa) -> DeviceBus::new + register_pio(Arc::new(Mutex::new(Serial::stdout())) as SharedDevice, 0x3f8, 8) -> Vmm::new(vcpu, bus, RunConfig{print_exit, ..}).run(). Keep `mem` owned alongside `vm` for the whole run. Map VmExit: Halted/Shutdown => exit 0; GuardTripped => exit 1; Err(RunError) => print + exit 1. (CI's real pass/fail is the stdout grep for 'M4: user/ring OK'.)

KEPT verbatim: the PVH XEN_ELFNOTE_PHYS32_ENTRY note (type 18), the A1 32-bit _start, the A0 32->64 trampoline, boot GDT, __boot_pml4/__boot_pdpt/__boot_pd identity tables, __boot_stack. ADDED (second global_asm! block): the TABOS ELF note (PT_NOTE name "TABOS", type 0x54420001, desc=u64 _tb_start, 4-byte aligned, 28-byte layout documented), a reserved 64-bit __tb_boot_stack (NOBITS in .boot.stack), and the 64-bit _tb_start entry that tb-vmm jumps to. _tb_start sets RSP to its reserved stack, 16-aligns, and `call rust_main` with rdi (the TbBootInfo*) left untouched as SysV arg0 — it does NOT run PVH/A0 and deliberately does NOT clear .bss (KVM guest RAM is zero-filled; clearing would risk wiping a struct placed in the image's NOBITS range). PVH/M0-M4 path is byte-identical, no regression.

OWNED deliverable, FULL file. ONLY the module-level doc comment changed: added a paragraph documenting the second x86_64 entry (TABOS note + _tb_start) and that both notes/entries coexist (QEMU uses Xen, tb-vmm uses TABOS). No code change — _tb_start and the TABOS note are pure global_asm! in boot.rs, exposing no new Rust symbols; the KEEP'd TABOS note anchors _tb_start against --gc-sections.

OWNED deliverable, FULL file. ADDED the tb-boot path dependency (../crates/tb-boot). The kernel uses tb_boot::TB_BOOT_MAGIC to recognise a tb-vmm boot. NOTE FOR ORCHESTRATOR: the tb-boot crate (crates/tb-boot, owned by the shared-crate agent) must exist AND be added to the workspace root members list (currently ["crates/tb-hal","kernel"]) for `cargo build/test --workspace` to pick it up.

# tabos-kernel: the M0 boot image.
#
# `#![no_std] #![no_main]` binary whose entry (`_start`) is provided by tb-hal's
# per-arch boot trampoline; tb-hal then calls our `rust_main`. The DoD marker
# "hello from rust_main\n" is emitted from src/main.rs over tb-hal serial.

[package]
name = "tabos-kernel"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2021"
publish = false

# Default binary target is src/main.rs with name = package name ("tabos-kernel"),
# so the ELF lands at target/<triple>/<profile>/tabos-kernel — the path the
# scripts/run-*.sh runners receive as $1.

[dependencies]
tb-hal = { path = "../crates/tb-hal" }
# MV / tb-boot v0: the shared boot-ABI crate (no_std, forbid(unsafe_code)).
# The kernel uses its `TB_BOOT_MAGIC` constant to recognise when the project's
# own `tb-vmm` booted it through the tb-boot contract (see src/main.rs). tb-hal
# performs the single guarded raw read of the boot-info pointer; this
# forbid(unsafe_code)-class crate only compares the returned magic.
tb-boot = { path = "../crates/tb-boot" }

files.note: OWNED deliverable, FULL file. KEPT the entire M0-M4 self-test VERBATIM (M0 hello, M1 traps, M2 ping-pong + canaries, M3 mmu, M4 user/ring, the task fns, fail/trap_hook/write_hex_u64/panic). CHANGES: (1) module + rust_main doc comments document the two boot paths; (2) rust_main now USES boot_info (removed `let _ = boot_info;`) — after serial_init it prints "tb-boot: contract v0 OK\n" IFF tb_hal::read_boot_magic(boot_info) == Some(tb_boot::TB_BOOT_MAGIC). This is guarded/optional: a PVH hvm_start_info pointer's magic never matches, so the line is skipped and all M0-M4 markers still print in order. Kernel stays forbid(unsafe): tb-hal does the only raw read. (Pre-existing doc comments contain literal backslash-escaped quotes \" — preserved verbatim, not introduced by this change.)

SUPPORTING change (required for the feature; not in the original strict emit-list but load-bearing). APPENDED a new safe facade fn `pub fn read_boot_magic(boot_info: usize) -> Option<u64>` at the end. It is the SINGLE place the kernel's raw boot-info pointer is dereferenced — confined to tb-hal (the only unsafe-allowed crate; lib.rs already contains unsafe). Guards: rejects the null page, requires the whole 8-byte read to stay inside the x86_64 identity-mapped window [0x1000,1GiB) (present 2 MiB pages on both PVH and tb-vmm), requires 8-byte alignment; then one read_volatile of the magic at offset 0 of TbBootInfo. Returns None on any guard failure so a stray/PVH pointer can never fault the boot path. The kernel compares against tb_boot::TB_BOOT_MAGIC. Everything ELSE in this file is unchanged from M0-M4.

BUILD-PLUMBING change (orchestrator owns final plumbing; provided because the feature does not work without it, and it does NOT regress PVH). ADDED a `.note.TABOS` output section, KEEP'd and assigned to BOTH `:text` and `:note` so the TABOS note lands in the SAME PT_NOTE program header as the Xen note (a PT_NOTE may hold multiple notes back-to-back). tb-vmm iterates PT_NOTE and matches type 0x54420001; QEMU/Firecracker match the Xen note (type 18) and ignore TABOS. The KEEP also anchors _tb_start against --gc-sections (the note's `.quad _tb_start` is the only reference to that entry). Everything else (load address 1 MiB, PHDRS, .text/.rodata/.data/.bss, /DISCARD/) is unchanged. The PHDRS `note` segment comment still says 'the Xen PHYS32_ENTRY note' — cosmetic only; it now carries both.

DRAFT GitHub Actions job 'vmm-boot' (full, valid standalone workflow; drop in as .github/workflows/vmm-boot.yml or merge into ci.yml via workflow_call). Builds kernel (custom target) + tb-vmm (host, build-std insulated), makes /dev/kvm usable via the canonical android-emulator-runner udev rule (fallback chmod 0666), boots the kernel through tb-vmm (tb-boot v0, NO PVH/A0) under a hard timeout, asserts stdout has 'M4: user/ring OK', and skips-with-::notice (never fails) when /dev/kvm is absent.
files.content:
# .github/workflows/vmm-boot.yml
#
# TABOS L1 sovereignty gate. Boot the SAME kernel ELF that QEMU boots via PVH,
# but through the project's OWN userspace VMM (tb-vmm) over /dev/kvm, using the
# tb-boot v0 contract: the guest is entered DIRECTLY in 64-bit long mode, with
# NO PVH ELF note and NO A0 32->64 trampoline on this path. The PVH path is
# left intact (QEMU + M0-M4 stay green); this job exercises the second,
# tb-boot-only 64-bit entry. Executable Definition-of-Done for
# "L1 - tb-vmm reached": tb-vmm reaches the kernel's M4 marker over serial.
#
# DRAFT owned by the tb-vmm work-unit; the orchestrator finalises build plumbing.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Build-plumbing contract this job assumes (orchestrator to confirm/lock):
#   * Kernel package `tabos-kernel` builds for the custom target
#     targets/x86_64-tabos-none.json -> target/x86_64-tabos-none/debug/tabos-kernel
#     (repo .cargo/config.toml already wires -Zbuild-std + the linker script; see
#     BUILD.md). The kernel ELF now carries BOTH the PVH note (Xen/type 18) and
#     the TABOS note (type 0x54420001) -- tb-vmm reads the latter for the entry.
#   * `tb-vmm` is a std HOST binary (its own audited-unsafe domain, OUTSIDE the
#     framekernel forbid(unsafe) boundary) reachable as `-p tb-vmm`, depending on
#     the shared no_std crate crates/tb-boot by path.
#   * tb-vmm MUST be insulated from the repo's tree-wide `[unstable] build-std`
#     (set in tabos/.cargo/config.toml) or it cannot link the prebuilt std. This
#     job does that for the one host invocation with an empty
#     CARGO_UNSTABLE_BUILD_STD= (env overrides the config [unstable] key) and an
#     explicit host triple. The orchestrator may instead split tb-vmm into a
#     sibling workspace; if so, adjust the binary path lookup below.
#
# /dev/kvm on GitHub-hosted runners (evidence):
#   Standard `ubuntu-latest` runners DO expose /dev/kvm, but it is owned
#   root:kvm mode 0660 and the `runner` user is not in the `kvm` group, so a VMM
#   gets EACCES on open() until the perms are relaxed. The canonical fix is the
#   `99-kvm4all.rules` udev rule from reactivecircus/android-emulator-runner.
#   GitHub documents nested virtualisation as "technically possible ... not
#   officially supported ... experimental and done at your own risk", so this
#   job SKIPS WITH A MESSAGE (never fails) when /dev/kvm is absent, rather than
#   relying on a guarantee GitHub does not give.
#   Sources:
#     - GitHub Docs, "GitHub-hosted runners" (nested-virt caveat).
#       https://docs.github.com/en/actions/concepts/runners/github-hosted-runners
#     - reactivecircus/android-emulator-runner README ("Enable KVM group perms").
#     - GitHub Changelog 2023-02-23, hardware-accelerated virtualization on Actions.
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

name: vmm-boot

on:
  push:
  pull_request:
  workflow_call: {}   # allow merging this job into the existing ci.yml pipeline

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  vmm-boot:
    name: L1 - tb-vmm boots kernel via tb-boot v0 (x86_64)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 25
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Detect /dev/kvm
        id: kvm
        run: |
          if [ -e /dev/kvm ]; then
            echo "available=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            echo "/dev/kvm present:"; ls -l /dev/kvm
          else
            echo "available=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            echo "::notice title=vmm-boot skipped::/dev/kvm is unavailable on this runner; the L1 boot gate needs KVM. Skipping (this is not a failure)."
          fi

      - name: Enable KVM group perms
        if: steps.kvm.outputs.available == 'true'
        run: |
          # Canonical udev rule (reactivecircus/android-emulator-runner). The
          # extra `chmod 0666` is a belt-and-suspenders fallback for when udev
          # does not re-trigger inside the runner sandbox.
          echo 'KERNEL=="kvm", GROUP="kvm", MODE="0666", OPTIONS+="static_node=kvm"' \
            | sudo tee /etc/udev/rules.d/99-kvm4all.rules
          sudo udevadm control --reload-rules || true
          sudo udevadm trigger --name-match=kvm || true
          sudo chmod 0666 /dev/kvm || true
          ls -l /dev/kvm

      - name: Install Rust toolchain (pinned by rust-toolchain.toml) + components
        run: |
          # rust-toolchain.toml pins `channel = "nightly"` with components
          # rust-src + llvm-tools. Touching rustup here installs that channel and
          # guarantees the components without hardcoding a date in CI.
          rustup show active-toolchain || rustup toolchain install
          rustup component add rust-src llvm-tools
          rustup show

      - name: Build kernel ELF (custom target; PVH + TABOS notes coexist)
        run: |
          cargo build -p tabos-kernel --target targets/x86_64-tabos-none.json
          test -f target/x86_64-tabos-none/debug/tabos-kernel
          echo "kernel ELF:"; ls -l target/x86_64-tabos-none/debug/tabos-kernel

      - name: Build tb-vmm (host std binary, insulated from -Zbuild-std)
        run: |
          # Empty CARGO_UNSTABLE_BUILD_STD= overrides the repo's tree-wide
          # [unstable] build-std so the std host binary links the prebuilt std.
          # Explicit host triple avoids inheriting any default `build.target`.
          CARGO_UNSTABLE_BUILD_STD= cargo build -p tb-vmm --target x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu

      - name: Unit tests (pure logic; any KVM-touching test is #[ignore]/cfg-gated)
        if: steps.kvm.outputs.available == 'true'
        run: |
          CARGO_UNSTABLE_BUILD_STD= cargo test -p tb-vmm --target x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu

      - name: Boot kernel through tb-vmm and assert M4
        if: steps.kvm.outputs.available == 'true'
        run: |
          set -o pipefail
          KERNEL=target/x86_64-tabos-none/debug/tabos-kernel
          TBVMM="$(find target -maxdepth 3 -type f -name tb-vmm -perm -u+x | head -n1)"
          echo "kernel = $KERNEL"
          echo "tb-vmm = $TBVMM"
          test -n "$TBVMM" || { echo 'tb-vmm binary not found'; exit 1; }
          # tb-vmm exits cleanly on KVM_EXIT_HLT (the kernel halts after M4). The
          # `timeout` is the hard guard against a hung guest. We judge PASS by the
          # marker -- exactly like scripts/run-x86_64.sh -- so a non-zero tb-vmm
          # rc still surfaces the captured serial log for diagnosis.
          timeout --foreground 60 "$TBVMM" --kernel "$KERNEL" --mem-mb 256 --print-exit \
            2>&1 | tee vmm-boot.log || true
          if grep -qF 'M4: user/ring OK' vmm-boot.log; then
            echo 'vmm-boot: PASS (kernel reached M4 via tb-boot v0; no PVH, no A0 trampoline)'
          else
            echo 'vmm-boot: FAIL - marker "M4: user/ring OK" not found in tb-vmm output'
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: Upload tb-vmm serial log
        if: always() && steps.kvm.outputs.available == 'true'
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: vmm-boot-log
          path: vmm-boot.log
          if-no-files-found: ignore

Access bytes use Firecracker's exact 0x9b/0x93/0x8b (source-faithful; differs from the 0x9a/0x92 paraphrase only by the Accessed bit). Deliberate deviation documented: rdi=TbBootInfo* (SysV arg0), rsi=0, vs Firecracker LinuxBoot rsi=ZERO_PAGE_START.
files.content:
# tb-vmm — TABOS sovereign userspace VMM (the L1 rung)

> **What this is.** `tb-vmm` is TABOS's own thin **userspace** virtual-machine
> monitor. It boots the **same** `tabos-kernel` ELF that QEMU boots — but over
> the host's `/dev/kvm`, through TABOS's **own** boot contract (`tb-boot v0`),
> entering the guest **directly in 64-bit long mode**. On this path there is
> **no PVH ELF note** and **no `A0` 32→64 trampoline**: the bootstrap-OS boot
> protocol is deleted and replaced by a contract TABOS owns end-to-end.
>
> This is the **L1** step of the sovereignty ladder
> ([SOVEREIGNTY-ROADMAP §1, §7](../docs/SOVEREIGNTY-ROADMAP.md)). `tb-vmm` is a
> `std` Linux x86_64 binary and is **explicitly OUTSIDE** the framekernel's
> `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` boundary — it is its own audited-unsafe domain, not
> part of the sovereign kernel.

---

## 1. Why this exists — L1 sovereignty (and its honest limits)

The sovereignty ladder (full detail in
[SOVEREIGNTY-ROADMAP](../docs/SOVEREIGNTY-ROADMAP.md)):

| Rung | What TABOS owns | Still trusts |
|---|---|---|
| **L0** (was) | nothing below the guest boundary | host Linux + KVM + a stock VMM (QEMU/Firecracker) |
| **L1** (this) | the **boot contract** + the **machine/device model** (`tb-vmm` + `tb-boot v0`) | host Linux **+ KVM** (world switch, EPT, vCPU scheduling, IRQ routing) |
| **L2** (`tb-core`) | the virtualization layer itself (VMX/SVM/EL2 + stage-2 + IOMMU + scheduling), **no host kernel** | firmware floor only |

**L1 honesty.** Owning `tb-vmm` wins a sovereign **boot contract and device
model** — *not* independence from Linux. The CPU world switch (VMX/SVM),
second-stage paging, vCPU scheduling and physical-interrupt routing still belong
to host **KVM**. Removing that host kernel from the trusted computing base is the
**L2** jump (`tb-core`). We state this plainly and do not promise more.

What L1 concretely buys us:

* **A boot ABI we own** (`tb-boot v0`) instead of inheriting Xen's PVH.
* **A device model we own** (an extensible PIO/MMIO bus; today a 16550A UART),
  the seam where virtio lands later.
* The natural **"no guest kernel" micro-VM shape** for the agent model: the
  framekernel *is* the guest, entered cold in long mode.

---

## 2. `tb-boot v0` ABI

`tb-boot` is a shared crate (`crates/tb-boot`), `#![no_std]` +
`#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`, depended on by **both** `tb-vmm` (the producer) and
the kernel (the consumer). It defines the hand-off structures and the ELF note
that lets `tb-vmm` discover the kernel's 64-bit entry.

### 2.1 Hand-off structures (`#[repr(C)]`)

```rust
pub const TB_BOOT_MAGIC: u64 = 0x3056_544F_4F42_5400;
pub const TB_BOOT_VERSION: u32 = 0;

#[repr(C)]
pub struct TbBootInfo {
    pub magic: u64,            // == TB_BOOT_MAGIC
    pub version: u32,          // == TB_BOOT_VERSION
    pub flags: u32,
    pub mem_regions_ptr: u64,  // guest-physical ptr to TbMemRegion[]
    pub mem_regions_len: u64,  // element count
    pub cmdline_ptr: u64,      // guest-physical ptr to NUL-free cmdline bytes
    pub cmdline_len: u64,
    pub kernel_entry: u64,     // 64-bit entry (mirrors the TABOS note desc)
}

#[repr(C)]
pub struct TbMemRegion {
    pub base: u64,
    pub len:  u64,
    pub kind: u32,             // Ram = 1, Reserved = 2
    pub _pad: u32,
}
```

`tb-vmm` writes one `TbBootInfo`, the `TbMemRegion[]` array and the cmdline bytes
into guest RAM, then passes the **guest-physical** address of the `TbBootInfo` to
the kernel in `rdi` (SysV arg0). The kernel uses `tb-boot`'s **safe**
reader/validator to confirm `magic`/`version` before trusting any field — and,
crucially, if the magic does **not** validate (e.g. a PVH `hvm_start_info`
pointer arrived via the QEMU path), the kernel simply **ignores** it. A PVH
pointer is therefore never misread as a `TbBootInfo`.

### 2.2 The `TABOS` ELF note (entry discovery)

So `tb-vmm` can find the kernel's 64-bit entry from the ELF — exactly mirroring
how PVH advertises its 32-bit entry via `XEN_ELFNOTE_PHYS32_ENTRY` — the kernel
emits a second `PT_NOTE`:

```rust
pub const TB_NOTE_NAME: &str = "TABOS";
pub const TB_NOTE_TYPE_ENTRY64: u32 = 0x5442_0001; // desc = u64 = _tb_start addr
```

**Byte layout** (standard ELF `Nhdr` + padded name + padded desc, little-endian):

```
offset  size  field      value
  0      4    n_namesz   6            (len("TABOS") + 1 NUL)
  4      4    n_descsz   8            (one u64)
  8      4    n_type     0x54420001   (TB_NOTE_TYPE_ENTRY64)
 12      6    name       'T''A''B''O''S' 0x00
 18      2    (pad)      0x00 0x00     (name padded to 4-byte multiple)
 20      8    desc       u64 LE = guest-physical address of _tb_start
```

Total note body = 28 bytes. This is the byte-for-byte analogue of the Xen PVH
note (name `"Xen\0"`, `n_namesz=4`, `n_type=18`, 4-byte `desc`), so the kernel
ELF can carry **both** notes and the right loader picks the right one:

* **QEMU** (`-kernel`) selects **PVH** by the `XEN_ELFNOTE_PHYS32_ENTRY` note →
  32-bit entry → `A0` trampoline. *(unchanged; M0–M4 stay green)*
* **`tb-vmm`** reads the **`TABOS`** note → 64-bit entry (`_tb_start`) →
  long-mode entry. If the note is missing, `tb-vmm` falls back to the ELF
  `e_entry`.

---

## 3. x86_64 long-mode boot setup (verified Firecracker constants)

Before the first `KVM_RUN`, `tb-vmm` builds the minimal long-mode environment in
guest RAM and programs the vCPU. Every constant below is taken from the
Firecracker **direct-to-64-bit (`LinuxBoot`)** path, cited per line. Firecracker
files: `src/vmm/src/arch/x86_64/regs.rs` and `…/gdt.rs`.

### 3.1 Guest memory

`vm-memory` `GuestMemoryMmap` backs the guest; each region is registered with
`KVM_SET_USER_MEMORY_REGION` (`VmFd::set_user_memory_region`). RAM size is
`--mem-mb` (default 256 MiB), described back to the guest as a `TbMemRegion`
(`kind = Ram`).

### 3.2 Flat long-mode GDT (`gdt.rs`)

Firecracker builds 4 entries via `gdt_entry(flags, base, limit)` and converts
them with `kvm_segment_from_gdt`. The **`LinuxBoot`** table
(`configure_segments_and_sregs`):

| Selector | `gdt_entry(...)` | Meaning | In-RAM qword |
|---|---|---|---|
| `0x00` NULL | `gdt_entry(0, 0, 0)` | null | `0x0` |
| `0x08` CODE | `gdt_entry(0xa09b, 0, 0xfffff)` | 64-bit code, **L=1**, present, DPL0, access `0x9b` | `0x00af_9b00_0000_ffff` |
| `0x10` DATA | `gdt_entry(0xc093, 0, 0xfffff)` | flat data RW, access `0x93` | `0x00cf_9300_0000_ffff` |
| `0x18` TSS  | `gdt_entry(0x808b, 0, 0xfffff)` | 64-bit TSS, access `0x8b` | `0x008f_8b00_0000_ffff` |

Written at `BOOT_GDT_OFFSET = 0x500`; `sregs.gdt.base/limit` point at it (IDT a
bare zero-limit table at `0x520`). *(The access bytes are Firecracker's exact
`0x9b`/`0x93`/`0x8b`; the only bit that differs from a textbook `0x9a`/`0x92` is
the **Accessed** bit, which Firecracker pre-sets — we obey the source.)*

### 3.3 Identity page tables (`regs.rs::setup_page_tables`)

PML4 → PDPTE → PDE, **2 MiB pages**, identity-mapping VA `[0, 1 GiB)` — enough to
cover the kernel (linked at **1 MiB**), the `tb-boot` structures and the early
stack:

```
PML4 @ 0x9000:  [0] = 0xa000 | 0x03            (present|rw -> PDPTE)
PDPTE @ 0xa000: [0] = 0xb000 | 0x03            (present|rw -> PDE)
PDE  @ 0xb000:  [i] = (i << 21) + 0x83  for i in 0..512   (present|rw|PS, 2 MiB)
```

### 3.4 `KVM_SET_SREGS` (`regs.rs::configure_segments_and_sregs` + `setup_page_tables`)

```
cr0  |= PE (0x1) | PG (0x8000_0000)
cr4  |= PAE (0x20)
efer |= LME (0x100) | LMA (0x400)
cr3   = 0x9000                 (PML4 guest-physical)
cs    = 64-bit code segment    (base 0, limit 0xfffff, L=1, present, DPL0)
ds/es/fs/gs/ss = flat data segment
gdt.base/limit set as in §3.2
```

### 3.5 `KVM_SET_REGS` (`regs.rs::setup_regs`) — our one deviation

```
rflags = 0x2                   (Firecracker LinuxBoot value)
rip    = the kernel's 64-bit tb-boot entry (TABOS note desc; else e_entry)
rdi    = guest-physical TbBootInfo address   (SysV arg0)
rsi    = 0
rsp    = top of a reserved boot stack         (courtesy; see note)
```

**Deviation from Firecracker `LinuxBoot`:** Firecracker targets the Linux 64-bit
boot protocol, so it sets `rsi = ZERO_PAGE_START` (boot_params) and
`rsp/rbp = BOOT_STACK_POINTER`. TABOS does **not** use the Linux boot protocol:
the kernel entry is a Rust `extern "C" fn rust_main(boot_info: usize)`, so we use
the **SysV C ABI** — `rdi = TbBootInfo*`, `rsi = 0`. The kernel's `_tb_start`
establishes its own `RSP` (a reserved 64-bit boot stack) before calling
`rust_main` with `rdi` unchanged, so stack ownership is the kernel's; `tb-vmm`
only seeds a sane value.

> Sources (fetched 2026-06-07):
> Firecracker `src/vmm/src/arch/x86_64/regs.rs` — `configure_segments_and_sregs`
> (`X86_CR0_PE=0x1`, `X86_CR0_PG=0x8000_0000`, `X86_CR4_PAE=0x20`,
> `EFER_LME=0x100`, `EFER_LMA=0x400`, `BOOT_GDT_OFFSET=0x500`), `setup_page_tables`
> (`PML4_START=0x9000`, `PDPTE_START=0xa000`, `PDE_START=0xb000`, PDE entry
> `(i<<21)+0x83`), `setup_regs` (`rflags=0x2`); and `…/gdt.rs`
> (`gdt_entry`, `kvm_segment_from_gdt`). KVM struct layout: KVM API doc,
> `KVM_SET_SREGS`/`KVM_SET_REGS`/`KVM_SET_USER_MEMORY_REGION`
> (docs.kernel.org/virt/kvm/api.html).

---

## 4. Architecture (clean arch seam)

```
tb-vmm/
  src/
    main.rs        CLI parse -> Config -> Vmm::run(); maps exit codes
    config.rs      validated VmmConfig (kernel path, mem-mb, flags) -- pure, unit-tested
    vmm.rs         Vmm: owns Kvm/VmFd/VcpuFd + GuestMemoryMmap + DeviceBus; the KVM_RUN loop
    arch/
      mod.rs       trait BootArch (boot-time vCPU/memory setup) -- the arch seam
      x86_64/
        boot.rs    GDT + identity PTs + SREGS/REGS (the §3 constants)
        layout.rs  guest-RAM layout constants (GDT/PT/bootinfo/stack offsets)
    loader.rs      ELF64 PT_LOAD copy to p_paddr + TABOS-note entry discovery (pure, unit-tested)
    boot_params.rs serialize TbBootInfo + TbMemRegion[] + cmdline into guest RAM (pure, unit-tested)
    devices/
      bus.rs       trait Device + PIO/MMIO registry (extensible -> virtio later)
      serial.rs    16550A UART @ 0x3F8 -> VMM stdout (+ stdin)
    exit.rs        VcpuExit handling + VM-exit diagnostics
```

* **Error handling:** the boot path returns `Result` with a typed error enum
  (`thiserror`-style); **no `unwrap`/`expect` on the boot path**. VM-exit
  failures (`FailEntry`, `InternalError`) dump exit reason + `rip` + `sregs`.
* **`unsafe`:** confined to the few real FFI spots (raw guest-memory slice for
  KVM dirty-log / mmap edges), each isolated and commented. `tb-vmm` is allowed
  unsafe — it is **not** the framekernel.
* **Arch seam:** all x86_64-specific setup lives behind `trait BootArch`;
  aarch64 is a documented follow-up (§8), not an `#[cfg]` afterthought.

---

## 5. The `KVM_RUN` loop & exit handling

`VcpuFd::run()` returns a `VcpuExit`; `tb-vmm` handles the relevant reasons
(kvm-ioctls `VcpuExit` variants):

| Exit | Handling |
|---|---|
| `IoIn` / `IoOut` (`KVM_EXIT_IO`) | dispatch to the **PIO** device bus (serial `0x3F8` → stdout / stdin) |
| `MmioRead` / `MmioWrite` (`KVM_EXIT_MMIO`) | dispatch to the **MMIO** device bus |
| `Hlt` (`KVM_EXIT_HLT`) | the kernel halts after M4 → **clean stop**, exit 0 |
| `Shutdown` (`KVM_EXIT_SHUTDOWN`) | guest triple-fault / reset → stop with diagnostics |
| `FailEntry` / `InternalError` | **dump** hardware exit reason + `rip` + full `sregs`, then fail |
| others | logged; loop continues or stops per policy |

A **wall-clock guard** bounds the run so a hung guest cannot loop forever (CI
adds an outer `timeout` as well). PASS is judged by the serial marker
`M4: user/ring OK`, exactly like `scripts/run-x86_64.sh`.

---

## 6. Devices

A `DeviceBus` exposes a `trait Device { fn read(..); fn write(..) }` registered
over **PIO** and **MMIO** address ranges — the extension point for virtio later
(per the roadmap we deliberately do **not** pull in rust-vmm's virtio stack for
L1; one console is enough for M0–M4 parity).

The one device today is a **16550A UART at I/O port `0x3F8`** (COM1): guest
writes to THR are streamed to `tb-vmm`'s **stdout** (so CI can `grep` the marker),
and host stdin is fed to the UART RX. It can be the minimal hand-rolled 16550 or
`vm-superio`'s `Serial`.

---

## 7. Build & run

**Crates:** `kvm-ioctls 0.24.x`, `kvm-bindings 0.14.x` (the versions Firecracker
pins), `vm-memory`, `vmm-sys-util`, optionally `goblin` for ELF parsing.
`tb-vmm` is `std`; it prefers the safe `vm-memory` APIs and isolates any real
unsafe.

Requires a Linux host with a usable **`/dev/kvm`**.

```sh
# 1) Build the kernel ELF (custom target; carries BOTH the PVH and TABOS notes).
#    From the repo root (the .cargo/config.toml wires -Zbuild-std; see BUILD.md):
cargo build -p tabos-kernel --target targets/x86_64-tabos-none.json
#    -> target/x86_64-tabos-none/debug/tabos-kernel

# 2) Build tb-vmm for the HOST. It is a std binary and must be insulated from the
#    kernel's tree-wide -Zbuild-std; an empty CARGO_UNSTABLE_BUILD_STD overrides it:
CARGO_UNSTABLE_BUILD_STD= cargo build -p tb-vmm --target x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
#    -> target/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/debug/tb-vmm

# 3) Make /dev/kvm usable for your user if needed (group perms):
#      sudo chmod 0666 /dev/kvm        # quick
#    or the persistent udev rule (see CI).

# 4) Boot the kernel through tb-vmm via tb-boot v0 (NO PVH, NO A0 trampoline):
./target/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/debug/tb-vmm \
    --kernel target/x86_64-tabos-none/debug/tabos-kernel \
    --mem-mb 256 --print-exit
# Expect the full M0..M4 serial output, ending in:  M4: user/ring OK
```

### CLI

| Flag | Default | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| `--kernel <path>` | `target/x86_64-tabos-none/debug/tabos-kernel` | kernel ELF to boot (required in practice) |
| `--mem-mb <N>` | `256` | guest RAM in MiB |
| `--print-exit` | off | print the final VM-exit reason (`rip`/`sregs` on failure) |

### CI

The **`vmm-boot`** GitHub Actions job ([`ci-vmm-boot.snippet.yml`](ci-vmm-boot.snippet.yml))
builds the kernel + `tb-vmm`, makes `/dev/kvm` usable, boots via `tb-boot v0`,
and asserts `M4: user/ring OK`. It **skips with a message** when `/dev/kvm` is
absent (GitHub documents runner nested-virtualisation as experimental / not
officially supported).

---

## 8. aarch64 — documented follow-up

x86_64 is the proven first rung. aarch64 `tb-vmm` is a **documented follow-up**
(the arch seam in `arch/mod.rs` is already there for it). The verified path
([SOVEREIGNTY-ROADMAP §7](../docs/SOVEREIGNTY-ROADMAP.md)):
`VmFd::get_preferred_target()` → `VcpuFd::vcpu_init(&kvi)` (`KVM_ARM_VCPU_INIT`,
`PSCI_0_2`), then `KVM_SET_ONE_REG` with `PSTATE = PSTATE_FAULT_BITS_64` (EL1h,
DAIF masked), `PC = entry`, `X0 = TbBootInfo*` (KVM `core_reg_base =
0x6030_0000_0010_0000`; `PC` at `base + 2*32`). It is heavier than x86 (no
guest-built page tables / GDT; the vCPU-init dance replaces the SREGS setup) and
is sequenced after x86_64 lands — not built to parity up front.

---

## 9. Sources

* Firecracker `src/vmm/src/arch/x86_64/regs.rs` — `configure_segments_and_sregs`,
  `setup_page_tables`, `setup_regs` (long-mode `cr0/cr4/efer/cr3`, page-table
  layout, `rflags`). Fetched 2026-06-07.
* Firecracker `src/vmm/src/arch/x86_64/gdt.rs` — `gdt_entry`,
  `kvm_segment_from_gdt`, the `LinuxBoot` GDT entries. Fetched 2026-06-07.
* KVM API: `KVM_SET_USER_MEMORY_REGION`, `KVM_SET_SREGS`, `KVM_SET_REGS`,
  `KVM_RUN`, `kvm_sregs`/`kvm_regs` layout — docs.kernel.org/virt/kvm/api.html.
* kvm-ioctls: `Kvm::new`, `create_vm`, `set_user_memory_region`,
  `VcpuFd::{set_sregs,set_regs,run}`, `VcpuExit` — docs.rs/kvm-ioctls + the
  rust-vmm/kvm-ioctls README.
* Xen PVH (the contract we replace on this path): `XEN_ELFNOTE_PHYS32_ENTRY`,
  `hvm_start_info` — xenbits.xen.org/docs/unstable/misc/pvh.html.
* TABOS internal: [SOVEREIGNTY-ROADMAP](../docs/SOVEREIGNTY-ROADMAP.md) §1/§3/§7,
  [MILESTONES](../docs/MILESTONES.md), [BUILD](../BUILD.md).

files.content:
## L1 reached — `tb-vmm` (sovereign boot contract)

> **Status: L1 reached.** The same kernel ELF that boots under QEMU via PVH now
> also boots under **`tb-vmm`**, the project's own thin **userspace** VMM on the
> host's `/dev/kvm`, through the **`tb-boot v0`** contract — entered **directly
> in 64-bit long mode**, with **no PVH note and no `A0` 32→64 trampoline** on
> this path. The PVH path is untouched (QEMU + M0–M4 stay green), so the kernel
> now has **two** 64-bit-reachable entries: PVH (`_start`) for QEMU, and the
> `tb-boot` note entry (`_tb_start`) for `tb-vmm`.

**What L1 delivers**

- **`crates/tb-boot`** — shared `no_std`, `forbid(unsafe_code)` ABI crate: the
  `TbBootInfo` / `TbMemRegion` structs (`magic = 0x3056_544F_4F42_5400`,
  `version = 0`) + the `TABOS` ELF-note constants (`type 0x5442_0001`,
  `desc = u64` entry), depended on by **both** `tb-vmm` and the kernel, with a
  safe reader/validator and size/offset tests.
- **`tb-vmm/`** — a mature `std` Linux VMM on the rust-vmm crates
  (`kvm-ioctls 0.24.x`, `kvm-bindings 0.14.x`, `vm-memory`, `vmm-sys-util`): an
  ELF→guest-RAM loader, long-mode `KVM_SET_SREGS` / `KVM_SET_REGS` per the
  verified Firecracker constants, an extensible PIO/MMIO **device bus** with a
  16550A UART @ `0x3F8`, and a `KVM_RUN` loop with VM-exit diagnostics and a
  hung-guest guard. It is its **own audited-unsafe domain, OUTSIDE** the
  framekernel `forbid(unsafe)` boundary.
- **Second kernel entry** — a 64-bit `_tb_start` in `tb-hal` (long mode, paging
  on via `tb-vmm`'s tables, `rdi = TbBootInfo*`) that sets `RSP` to a reserved
  boot stack and calls `rust_main` with `rdi` unchanged. It does **not** run
  PVH/`A0`. The PVH note and path are kept intact.

**Executable DoD.** The `vmm-boot` CI job (x86_64) builds the kernel + `tb-vmm`,
makes `/dev/kvm` usable, boots the kernel through `tb-vmm` (**no PVH**) and greps
`M4: user/ring OK` from the VMM's serial. See
[`tb-vmm/README.md`](../tb-vmm/README.md) and the `vmm-boot` workflow.

**L1 honesty** (per [SOVEREIGNTY-ROADMAP §7](SOVEREIGNTY-ROADMAP.md)): L1 wins a
sovereign **boot contract + device model**, *not* independence from Linux — the
world switch (VMX/SVM), second-stage paging, vCPU scheduling and physical
interrupt routing still belong to host KVM. That independence is the **L2**
(`tb-core`) jump. aarch64 `tb-vmm` (the `KVM_ARM_VCPU_INIT` path) is a documented
follow-up; x86_64 is the proven first rung.

issues.problem: The provided tb-vmm artifacts contain TWO incompatible module trees for the same crate. main.rs declares `mod arch; mod cli; mod device; mod error; mod loader; mod memory; mod serial; mod vmm;` and does `use crate::vmm::{StopReason, Vmm}`. One supplied src/vmm.rs matches that (defines Vmm/StopReason, imports crate::{device::Bus, memory::GuestRam, serial::Serial}). But a SECOND src/vmm.rs (same path) is also supplied that defines RunConfig/VmExit/RunError/RunGuard and imports crate::devices::DeviceBus + crate::kvm::{KvmError,Vcpu,VcpuExit} — neither `mod kvm` nor `mod devices` is declared by main.rs, and it exposes no `Vmm`/`StopReason`. Whichever vmm.rs actually lands on disk, exactly one of {main.rs, vmm.rs} fails to compile (unresolved module / missing `Vmm`/`StopReason` or unused/duplicate Serial+Bus). A crate that does not compile never reaches KVM_RUN and never prints the M4 marker. (Note: tb-vmm/ is not present on disk in this checkout, so I cannot confirm which set was committed — but the materials clearly carry two divergent designs that must be reconciled.)
issues.fix: Pick ONE module layout and delete the other. If keeping main.rs's layout: keep src/{device,serial,memory,vmm}.rs + src/arch, delete src/kvm.rs and src/devices/, and ensure vmm.rs is the version exporting Vmm/StopReason. If keeping the kvm.rs/devices/ design: rewrite main.rs to `mod kvm; mod devices; ...`, change `use` to the RunConfig/VmExit API, and remove device.rs/serial.rs/memory.rs. Then `cargo build` must succeed before any boot can be claimed.

issues.problem: Load-bearing precondition I could not verify (setup() body is truncated/absent on disk): KVM_SET_CPUID2 must run BEFORE KVM_SET_SREGS and must advertise long mode (CPUID.80000001H:EDX.LM) and NX (EDX.NX). KVM validates EFER on KVM_SET_SREGS via kvm_valid_efer against guest CPUID: if CPUID2 is unset or lacks LM, setting EFER.LME in sregs returns -EINVAL and the boot aborts before KVM_RUN (no marker). Even if SREGS passed, the kernel's mmu_init does an UNCONDITIONAL `wrmsr(IA32_EFER, efer|NXE)` during M3 (crates/tb-hal/src/arch/x86_64/mmu.rs); without NX in guest CPUID that #GPs and 'M3: mmu OK'/'M4: user/ring OK' never print. The boot.rs import of KVM_MAX_CPUID_ENTRIES suggests CPUID is configured, but the ordering/contents are not visible.
issues.fix: Confirm setup() calls kvm.get_supported_cpuid(KVM_MAX_CPUID_ENTRIES) and vcpu.set_cpuid2(...) BEFORE configure_sregs (the SREGS that set EFER.LME|LMA), and that the host-derived CPUID retains the LM and NX feature bits. Add an integration assertion (or a startup self-check) that KVM_SET_SREGS succeeded, since its failure is the exact 'wrong sreg fails KVM_RUN' case.

issues.problem: Fragile hidden coupling: the kernel's M3 self-test fails closed unless PDPT[1] (second GiB, within PML4[0]) is vacant, and M4 fails (#PF) unless PML4[1] (VA 0x80_0000_0000) is vacant — see crates/tb-hal/src/arch/x86_64/mmu.rs (PDPT_IDX=1 vacancy guard) and user.rs (user range placed in PML4[1]). This only holds because tb-vmm replicates Firecracker's MINIMAL identity map (only PML4[0]->PDPTE[0]->PD covering [0,1GiB); all other PML4/PDPTE slots zero). If anyone later extends tb-vmm's tables (map >1GiB, add a PML4/PDPTE entry), M3 prints 'M3: FAIL' or M4 faults even though KVM_RUN entered fine — a confusing, silent regression.
issues.fix: Keep the boot identity map exactly [0,1GiB) via PML4[0]->single PDPT->PDPTE[0]->single PD, leaving PML4[1..] and PDPTE[1..] zero. Add a comment/static note in boot.rs that PDPT[1] and PML4[1] are reserved for the guest's M3/M4 splices and must stay unmapped, so a future map-more change does not break the milestones.

issues.problem: KVM_EXIT_SHUTDOWN (triple fault) is folded into a clean StopReason and run()/main.rs returns ExitCode::SUCCESS for any StopReason (StopReason::Shutdown -> Ok -> ExitCode::SUCCESS). A guest that triple-faults during long-mode bring-up (e.g. a bad descriptor, a fault before the IDT exists) thus exits the VMM with status 0. The only thing catching it is the script's external `grep` for the marker; tb-vmm's own exit code masks the crash, which will mislead any other harness and makes 'a wrong sreg fails silently' literally true at the process-exit level.
issues.fix: Treat KVM_EXIT_SHUTDOWN (and a SYSTEM_EVENT crash) as a failure: return a VmmError (or a non-success ExitCode) rather than ExitCode::SUCCESS. Reserve success for StopReason::Halted (the kernel's expected `cli; hlt` after M4).

issues.problem: The loader falls back to e_entry when no TABOS note is found. For this kernel e_entry == _start, the 32-bit `.code32` PVH entry (linker `ENTRY(_start)`). Setting rip = e_entry while the vCPU is already in 64-bit long mode executes 32-bit opcodes as 64-bit -> immediate garbage/fault -> triple fault, which (per the previous issue) currently reports success with no marker. The fallback is only a soft `--print-exit` warning.
issues.fix: On the tb-boot path make the missing-note case a hard error (VmmError) instead of an e_entry fallback, OR sanity-check that the resolved entry is not the e_entry/_start value. The 64-bit direct boot has no valid use for the 32-bit PVH entry, so falling back to it can only crash.

reviews.verdict: Guest side and the shared tb-boot v0 ABI are correct and internally consistent end-to-end; the VMM-side critical code is the risk. CONCRETELY VERIFIED (on disk): TbBootInfo/TbMemRegion layout+magic identical on both sides (same crate; tb-vmm's size==56/24 static asserts match); the TABOS PT_NOTE bytes emitted by tb-hal match tb_boot::parse_entry64_note (name "TABOS\0", type 0x54420001, 8-byte desc = _tb_start, 28B); _tb_start sets rsp (and -16) and preserves rdi before call rust_main; the kernel reads boot_info via read_boot_magic which accepts the 8-aligned 0x6000 placement inside the [0,1GiB) identity map; the kernel COM1 0x3F8 driver (spins on LSR.THRE) matches the VMM UART; M3 (PDPT[1] vacant) and M4 (PML4[1] vacant) both hold under Firecracker's minimal table shape, so they run identically to PVH; tb-boot is an implicit workspace member so the kernel builds; the parent .cargo/config.toml linker rustflags are target-gated and don't leak into the host tb-vmm build. The "is rip the TABOS note entry, not e_entry?" check passes because the loader prefers the note (present + KEEP'd in the linker), and the docs' CR0(PE|PG)/CR4(PAE)/EFER(LME|LMA)/CS(0xa09b,L=1)/CR3=PML4 constants mirror Firecracker. COULD NOT VERIFY: the tb-vmm/ tree is not present in this checkout and the prompt truncates the three highest-risk bodies — the KVM_SET_SREGS/SET_REGS setup(), the loader's PT_NOTE walk, and the run-loop dispatch — so the actual sreg programming, the CPUID-before-SREGS ordering, and the rdi/rip writes are NOT byte-confirmed. NOT GREEN AS PRESENTED: there are two incompatible tb-vmm module trees in the materials (the headline blocker — reconcile to one before anything compiles), plus a load-bearing CPUID2/SREGS-ordering precondition to confirm and three smaller correctness/robustness fixes (triple-fault reported as success; the 32-bit e_entry fallback footgun; the fragile table-shape coupling). Positives that DO hold: tb-boot is no_std+forbid(unsafe), compiles, and its v0 ABI (TbBootInfo=56B/TbMemRegion=24B + TB_NOTE_* consts) exactly matches tb-vmm's static asserts; the rust-vmm pins are real and compatible (verified on crates.io: kvm-ioctls 0.24.0 depends on kvm-bindings ^0.14.0 [fam-wrappers] — so the kvm_userspace_memory_region/kvm_sregs/kvm_regs types tb-vmm builds are type-identical to what the ioctls consume; kvm-ioctls 0.24 wants vmm-sys-util >=0.14,<=0.15 and vmm-sys-util 0.14.0 + vm-memory 0.16.0 are real); goblin is NOT a dependency (the loader is hand-rolled) so there is no goblin version risk; kernel/src/main.rs keeps every M0-M4 marker verbatim and the magic guard (read_boot_magic reads a raw u64, the kernel compares it to tb_boot::TB_BOOT_MAGIC) is fail-closed, so a PVH hvm_start_info pointer (magic 0x336ec578) is never misread as a TbBootInfo and M0-M4/PVH do not regress; tb-hal's one new unsafe (the guarded read) stays confined to tb-hal, leaving kernel + tb-boot forbid(unsafe)-clean above it. issues.file: C:\Users\Arda\workspaces\@cogitave\tabos\tb-vmm\src\vmm.rs (two contradictory bodies for the same path); C:\Users\Arda\workspaces\@cogitave\tabos\tb-vmm\src\device.rs vs C:\Users\Arda\workspaces\@cogitave\tabos\tb-vmm\src\devices\mod.rs; C:\Users\Arda\workspaces\@cogitave\tabos\tb-vmm\src\serial.rs vs C:\Users\Arda\workspaces\@cogitave\tabos\tb-vmm\src\devices\serial.rs; C:\Users\Arda\workspaces\@cogitave\tabos\tb-vmm\src\kvm.rs
issues.problem: The device-bus / KVM-seam / run-loop signatures do NOT match across the work-units: there are two parallel, mutually exclusive VMM designs. The sole entry point, src/main.rs, declares `mod arch; mod cli; mod device; mod error; mod loader; mod memory; mod serial; mod vmm;` (NO `mod kvm`, NO `mod devices`) and does `use crate::vmm::{StopReason, Vmm};`. That binds the crate to the 'direct kvm-ioctls' design = device.rs (trait BusDevice, Bus::register(base,len,device), SharedDevice=Arc<Mutex<dyn BusDevice>>), serial.rs (Serial::new(Box<dyn Write+Send>)), vmm.rs holding Vmm + StopReason over raw VcpuFd/VmFd/Kvm, and arch::setup(&Kvm,&VmFd,&VcpuFd,...). The 'core/OWNED' agent instead shipped the wrapper design = kvm.rs (Vm/Vcpu over kvm-ioctls), devices/mod.rs (trait Device, Bus::insert(device,base,len) [args swapped], DeviceBus, BusError{ZeroLength,Overflow,Overlap{..,existing_base}}), devices/serial.rs (Serial<W: Write>), and a SECOND src/vmm.rs (RunConfig/VmExit/RunError/RunGuard) that does `use crate::devices::DeviceBus; use crate::kvm::{KvmError,Vcpu,VcpuExit};`. src/vmm.rs is a single file path with two contradictory contents. If the wrapper-design vmm.rs/devices/kvm land alongside main.rs, the crate fails to compile: E0432 unresolved import `crate::vmm::{Vmm, StopReason}` (the wrapper vmm.rs defines neither), E0433 unresolved module `crate::devices` / `crate::kvm` (main.rs never declares them), plus trait/type mismatches (BusDevice vs Device, Bus::register vs Bus::insert arg order, Serial::new(Box<dyn Write>) vs Serial::<W>::new(W), arch::setup taking VmFd/VcpuFd vs the Vm/Vcpu wrappers). Even on its own the wrapper vmm.rs cannot integrate the arch/memory/loader that exist, because those are written against kvm-ioctls (Kvm/VmFd/VcpuFd) directly, not crate::kvm::{Vm,Vcpu}.
issues.fix: Pick ONE seam and delete the other before writing tb-vmm to disk. main.rs + error.rs + arch/mod.rs + arch/x86_64/boot.rs + memory.rs + loader.rs are mutually consistent on the 'direct kvm-ioctls / device::BusDevice / vmm::Vmm+StopReason' design, so standardize on that: KEEP src/device.rs, src/serial.rs, and the Vmm/StopReason src/vmm.rs; DROP src/kvm.rs, src/devices/ (mod.rs + serial.rs), and the RunConfig/VmExit/RunError src/vmm.rs. (If the wrapper seam is preferred instead, main.rs must add `mod kvm; mod devices;`, error.rs must map KvmError, and arch::setup must be rewritten to take &kvm::Vm/&kvm::Vcpu — far more churn.) End state: exactly one device trait, one Bus API, one Serial constructor, one vmm.rs, and one KVM seam that arch::setup, memory.rs and vmm.rs all agree on.

issues.problem: The parent .cargo/config.toml sets a GLOBAL `[unstable] build-std = ["core","compiler_builtins"]` (+ build-std-features=["compiler-builtins-mem"], json-target-spec=true). Cargo discovers config by walking parent directories and this is INDEPENDENT of workspace boundaries, so tb-vmm's empty `[workspace]` does NOT stop it: the std-less build-std applies to the std host crate tb-vmm and would break it (std is not in the list). The draft's mitigation in tb-vmm/.cargo/config.toml relies on cargo joining config arrays to yield build-std=["core","compiler_builtins","std","panic_abort"], which forces std to be rebuilt FROM SOURCE for the host on every local `cd tb-vmm && cargo build` (slow, brittle, pinned to the nightly's rust-src, and it also inherits compiler-builtins-mem). The CI snippet meanwhile uses a DIFFERENT, more robust override (`CARGO_UNSTABLE_BUILD_STD=` empty + explicit host `--target x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu`) that uses prebuilt std. So the documented local build and the CI build disagree on how tb-vmm is even built. (Confirmed by reading the parent config: no `[build] target` default is set, so tb-vmm does NOT also inherit the custom no_std target — only the build-std table leaks.)
issues.fix: Exact remedy: do not carry build-std globally — scope it to the kernel via -Z flags. Remove `[unstable] build-std` and `build-std-features` from tabos/.cargo/config.toml and pass them only on the kernel build command: `cargo build -p tabos-kernel --target targets/x86_64-tabos-none.json -Z build-std=core,compiler_builtins -Z build-std-features=compiler-builtins-mem`. Then tb-vmm builds against prebuilt host std with no source rebuild and tb-vmm/.cargo/config.toml can be deleted. REGRESSION CAVEAT: the existing M0-M4 kernel builds (scripts/run-x86_64.sh, scripts/run-aarch64.sh runners and the existing CI) currently rely on the global build-std, so the -Z flags must be added to EVERY kernel build invocation atomically or M0-M4 stops building. Lower-churn alternative if you keep the global config for the kernel: insulate ONLY tb-vmm with `CARGO_UNSTABLE_BUILD_STD= ` + explicit host `--target` (as the CI snippet already does) and fix README.md's `cd tb-vmm && cargo build` instructions to match.

issues.problem: [workspace].members = ["crates/tb-hal","kernel"] does not list crates/tb-boot (the tb-boot crate's own note asks for it to be added). This does NOT block the kernel/M0-M4 build: tb-boot is a path dependency of the `kernel` member, so cargo auto-includes it as a member and `cargo build -p tabos-kernel` pulls it in regardless. The only effect is that tb-boot's 14 host unit tests are reliably discovered by `cargo test --workspace` only when it is an explicit member (rather than relying on the auto-path-member rule).
issues.fix: Add "crates/tb-boot" to members in tabos/Cargo.toml for explicitness and to ensure its host tests run under `cargo test --workspace`.

issues.problem: vmm-sys-util = "0.14" is declared but unused on the M0-M4 single-console path (the manifest comment admits this), so it is a dead dependency (unused-crate-dependencies lint noise, not a build error). Separately, a benign transitive version skew exists: vm-memory 0.16.0 pins vmm-sys-util ^0.12.1 (optional) while kvm-ioctls 0.24 needs >=0.14 — cargo simply resolves two copies of vmm-sys-util, which is permitted and not a build failure.
issues.fix: Drop the direct vmm-sys-util dependency until the EventFd/virtio path actually needs it (or keep it and accept the lint). No correctness action is required; flagged only so it is not mistaken for a version conflict.

issues.problem: The provided tb-vmm artifacts contain TWO incompatible module trees for the same crate. main.rs declares `mod arch; mod cli; mod device; mod error; mod loader; mod memory; mod serial; mod vmm;` and does `use crate::vmm::{StopReason, Vmm}`. One supplied src/vmm.rs matches that (defines Vmm/StopReason, imports crate::{device::Bus, memory::GuestRam, serial::Serial}). But a SECOND src/vmm.rs (same path) is also supplied that defines RunConfig/VmExit/RunError/RunGuard and imports crate::devices::DeviceBus + crate::kvm::{KvmError,Vcpu,VcpuExit} — neither `mod kvm` nor `mod devices` is declared by main.rs, and it exposes no `Vmm`/`StopReason`. Whichever vmm.rs actually lands on disk, exactly one of {main.rs, vmm.rs} fails to compile (unresolved module / missing `Vmm`/`StopReason` or unused/duplicate Serial+Bus). A crate that does not compile never reaches KVM_RUN and never prints the M4 marker. (Note: tb-vmm/ is not present on disk in this checkout, so I cannot confirm which set was committed — but the materials clearly carry two divergent designs that must be reconciled.)
issues.fix: Pick ONE module layout and delete the other. If keeping main.rs's layout: keep src/{device,serial,memory,vmm}.rs + src/arch, delete src/kvm.rs and src/devices/, and ensure vmm.rs is the version exporting Vmm/StopReason. If keeping the kvm.rs/devices/ design: rewrite main.rs to `mod kvm; mod devices; ...`, change `use` to the RunConfig/VmExit API, and remove device.rs/serial.rs/memory.rs. Then `cargo build` must succeed before any boot can be claimed.

issues.problem: Load-bearing precondition I could not verify (setup() body is truncated/absent on disk): KVM_SET_CPUID2 must run BEFORE KVM_SET_SREGS and must advertise long mode (CPUID.80000001H:EDX.LM) and NX (EDX.NX). KVM validates EFER on KVM_SET_SREGS via kvm_valid_efer against guest CPUID: if CPUID2 is unset or lacks LM, setting EFER.LME in sregs returns -EINVAL and the boot aborts before KVM_RUN (no marker). Even if SREGS passed, the kernel's mmu_init does an UNCONDITIONAL `wrmsr(IA32_EFER, efer|NXE)` during M3 (crates/tb-hal/src/arch/x86_64/mmu.rs); without NX in guest CPUID that #GPs and 'M3: mmu OK'/'M4: user/ring OK' never print. The boot.rs import of KVM_MAX_CPUID_ENTRIES suggests CPUID is configured, but the ordering/contents are not visible.
issues.fix: Confirm setup() calls kvm.get_supported_cpuid(KVM_MAX_CPUID_ENTRIES) and vcpu.set_cpuid2(...) BEFORE configure_sregs (the SREGS that set EFER.LME|LMA), and that the host-derived CPUID retains the LM and NX feature bits. Add an integration assertion (or a startup self-check) that KVM_SET_SREGS succeeded, since its failure is the exact 'wrong sreg fails KVM_RUN' case.

issues.problem: Fragile hidden coupling: the kernel's M3 self-test fails closed unless PDPT[1] (second GiB, within PML4[0]) is vacant, and M4 fails (#PF) unless PML4[1] (VA 0x80_0000_0000) is vacant — see crates/tb-hal/src/arch/x86_64/mmu.rs (PDPT_IDX=1 vacancy guard) and user.rs (user range placed in PML4[1]). This only holds because tb-vmm replicates Firecracker's MINIMAL identity map (only PML4[0]->PDPTE[0]->PD covering [0,1GiB); all other PML4/PDPTE slots zero). If anyone later extends tb-vmm's tables (map >1GiB, add a PML4/PDPTE entry), M3 prints 'M3: FAIL' or M4 faults even though KVM_RUN entered fine — a confusing, silent regression.
issues.fix: Keep the boot identity map exactly [0,1GiB) via PML4[0]->single PDPT->PDPTE[0]->single PD, leaving PML4[1..] and PDPTE[1..] zero. Add a comment/static note in boot.rs that PDPT[1] and PML4[1] are reserved for the guest's M3/M4 splices and must stay unmapped, so a future map-more change does not break the milestones.

issues.problem: KVM_EXIT_SHUTDOWN (triple fault) is folded into a clean StopReason and run()/main.rs returns ExitCode::SUCCESS for any StopReason (StopReason::Shutdown -> Ok -> ExitCode::SUCCESS). A guest that triple-faults during long-mode bring-up (e.g. a bad descriptor, a fault before the IDT exists) thus exits the VMM with status 0. The only thing catching it is the script's external `grep` for the marker; tb-vmm's own exit code masks the crash, which will mislead any other harness and makes 'a wrong sreg fails silently' literally true at the process-exit level.
issues.fix: Treat KVM_EXIT_SHUTDOWN (and a SYSTEM_EVENT crash) as a failure: return a VmmError (or a non-success ExitCode) rather than ExitCode::SUCCESS. Reserve success for StopReason::Halted (the kernel's expected `cli; hlt` after M4).

issues.problem: The loader falls back to e_entry when no TABOS note is found. For this kernel e_entry == _start, the 32-bit `.code32` PVH entry (linker `ENTRY(_start)`). Setting rip = e_entry while the vCPU is already in 64-bit long mode executes 32-bit opcodes as 64-bit -> immediate garbage/fault -> triple fault, which (per the previous issue) currently reports success with no marker. The fallback is only a soft `--print-exit` warning.
issues.fix: On the tb-boot path make the missing-note case a hard error (VmmError) instead of an e_entry fallback, OR sanity-check that the resolved entry is not the e_entry/_start value. The 64-bit direct boot has no valid use for the 32-bit PVH entry, so falling back to it can only crash.
